title: Toolbars: Add align="center"
rationale: Facts: Team member E Nelson requests center alignment support for bslib toolbars; references toolbar_badge request (#1316). Filed as a QoL enhancement. Classification: Feature enhancement — adds a layout option to the toolbar component. Duplicate check: No duplicate found among bslib toolbar issues (#1316, #1247, #1249, #1257, #1292). Wrong location: No — bslib owns toolbar layout. Regression: N/A. Reproduction plan: N/A (enhancement). Impact: Low — cosmetic alignment option; no user breakage; narrow scope. Already labeled Priority: Low in prior run (2026-05-21). Priority: Low confirmed. Next action: Add ai-triage:accepted to finalize.

title: Toolbar: Add `toolbar_badge()`
rationale: Facts: Garrick Aden-Buie (maintainer) requests a toolbar_badge() function using Bootstrap badge component for status text in toolbars. Points to Bootstrap badges docs. Classification: Planned feature from team; narrow scope enhancement. Duplicate check: No duplicate found. Wrong location: No — bslib is the correct repo. Regression: N/A. Reproduction plan: N/A. Impact: Low — enables status badges in toolbars; no user breakage. Already labeled Priority: Low in prior run (2026-05-21). Priority: Low confirmed. Next action: Add ai-triage:accepted to finalize.

title: Suggestion cards in nested lists
rationale: Facts: Garrick Aden-Buie (maintainer) requests support for suggestion card spans inside nested markdown lists, enabling hierarchical suggestion groups (e.g. in querychat-style apps). Originated as a comment from @cpsievert in #219. Classification: UI enhancement for suggestion card rendering. Duplicate check: Issue #219 covers flat suggestion lists; this is a distinct nested-list extension. Wrong location: No — shinychat owns suggestion card rendering. Regression: N/A. Impact: Low — improves UX for suggestion cards in nested contexts; no user breakage. Already labeled Priority: Low in prior run (2026-05-21). Priority: Low confirmed. Next action: Add ai-triage:accepted to finalize.

title: [Feature]: Pass additional parameters from `navset_card_*()` to `card()`
rationale: Facts: S. L. Carter (Steven314) reports that navset_card_pill() and related navset_card_*() functions cannot accept additional kwargs (e.g. height, style, fill) that would be passed through to the underlying card(). Provides a full runnable reproducible example in Python 3.14.5 / Shiny 1.6.2 on Windows 11. Workaround: wrap navset_card_*() in ui.div(height='auto'). Scope labeled 'Quality of Life' by reporter. Classification: Feature request / enhancement — well-defined, concrete, with a working workaround. Duplicate check: Issue #1451 (closed) is about missing full_screen option in navset_card_* — related pattern but distinct parameter. No prior issue found for kwargs passthrough to navset_card_*. Wrong location: No — py-shiny owns these UI components. Regression: No. Reproduction plan: Provided by reporter; embed navset_card_pill() inside a card() with fill context and observe constrained height. Impact: Low — QoL improvement; clear workaround exists; affects users building nested card/tab layouts. Priority: Low. Next action: Label Priority: Low, ai-triage:accepted.

title: Check for `stop_reason: max_tokens` when using structured data
rationale: Facts: Daniel Chen (chendaniely) reports that chat_structured() raises a cryptic JSONDecodeError when the model response is truncated by the default max_tokens limit. The JSON is cut off at 20679 chars. Workaround: pass kwargs={'max_tokens': 16000} to chat_structured(). Setting max_tokens too high (32000) triggers a separate ValueError about streaming being required. Root cause: stop_reason == 'max_tokens' causes orjson.loads() to fail on truncated JSON in _provider_anthropic.py line 811; the code does not check stop_reason before parsing. Full traceback provided. Classification: Bug — the library should detect stop_reason: max_tokens and surface a descriptive error rather than exposing a raw JSONDecodeError. Duplicate check: No prior chatlas issue found for this error pattern. Wrong location: No — chatlas owns chat_structured() and the Anthropic provider integration. Regression: No version comparison provided; this is a UX gap that may have existed since the feature shipped. Reproduction plan: Call chat_structured() with a large document that exceeds the default max_tokens for the model; observe JSONDecodeError at the orjson.loads call. Impact: Medium — affects any user extracting structured data from large documents; error message is misleading and workaround is non-obvious. Priority: Medium — valid bug, moderate impact, workaround available. Next action: Label Priority: Medium, ai-triage:accepted.

title: Optionally allow limiting streaming text speed at a maximum rate/speed
rationale: Facts: Bastián Olea Herera (bastianolea) requests an optional argument to cap the streaming text display speed for production apps with many concurrent users, to pace user interactions and reduce token burn. Reporter notes this is 'only a cosmetic or UI change.' No code or repro provided (feature request). Classification: Feature request — UI/cosmetic enhancement to add an optional max streaming rate. Duplicate check: No prior shinychat issue found for streaming speed throttling. Wrong location: No — shinychat owns the chat streaming UI. Regression: N/A. Reproduction plan: N/A (feature request). Impact: Low — niche use case for production multi-user apps; purely cosmetic; no bug or breakage. Priority: Low. Next action: Label Priority: Low, ai-triage:accepted.

title: Minor: Broken hyperlink for Posit image at the bottom of the docs site
rationale: Facts: E Nelson (elnelson575) reports the Posit logo image in the copyright statement at the bottom of the shinychat docs site is broken. No screenshot attached but the title says 'broken hyperlink for Posit image.' Classification: Docs/UI bug — broken image asset in docs footer. Duplicate check: No prior issue found for this. Wrong location: No — shinychat owns its docs site. Regression: Unknown when breakage occurred; no version comparison. Reproduction plan: Visit shinychat docs site footer and check Posit logo image renders. Impact: Low — purely cosmetic docs issue with no effect on library functionality. Priority: Low. Next action: Label Priority: Low, ai-triage:accepted.
